title: fragaria virginiana var. terrae-novae
intro: Fragaria virginiana var. terrae-novae, also known as New World wild strawberry or New World woodland strawberry, is a subspecies of the Fragaria virginiana plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its high content of salicylic acid and anthocyanins. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving cognitive function, and supporting the immune system. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
